summary: Gave the manifest `template: true` flag a real sync semantic: on a per-file basis, the sync tool now skips copy when the entry is template-managed AND the destination file already exists. Combined with flipping `delete_stale` to false on the two directory template entries (`automation/prompts/` and `automation/examples/`), consumer overrides AND consumer-added files in those directories now survive `--sync`. Added two override-durability tests; updated docs to describe the new first-time-only semantics and remove the previous workaround paths.

behavior_change.before: delete_stale: false alone did NOT preserve overrides because sync_entries always overwrote source-matched destination files via shutil.copyfile. The template flag was documentation-only.
behavior_change.after: template: true now means 'first-time-only sync': per file, when the entry is template AND the destination already exists, both copy and stale removal are skipped. Combined with delete_stale: false on prompts/ and examples/, consumer overrides and consumer-added files in those directories now persist across --sync.
behavior_change.implementation: Two small changes in Tools/repo-automation-sync.sh: (1) per-file copy loop adds `if entry.template and target.exists(): continue`. (2) stale_files() guard becomes `if entry.kind != 'directory' or not entry.delete_stale or entry.template: return []`.

tests_added: test_consumer_prompt_override_survives_resync: writes a sentinel into base.md, commits, runs --sync, asserts the sentinel still appears in base.md.
test_consumer_added_prompt_file_survives_resync: adds automation/prompts/consumer-custom.md, commits, runs --sync, asserts the file and its content remain.
behavior_proven: Per-file copy in sync_entries respects entry.template + target.exists().
stale_files() returns [] for template entries even when delete_stale is true, eliminating accidental removal of consumer overrides via the cleanup path.
Consumer flow: edit -> commit -> --sync -> content preserved.
Consumer-added files in template directories survive --sync.
consumer_constraints_now_documented: Owlory updates to template files (base.md, slice.md, review.md, example queue, example handoff, pyrightconfig.json) no longer auto-propagate to a consumer who already has those files. A consumer who wants the upstream content must delete the local file and re-sync, or merge manually.
Non-template entries (supervisor, context builder, schemas, harness test, docs, sync tool itself, automation/README.md, reusable-manifest.json) remain authoritative on every sync.
gaps_named_for_follow_up: Workflow templates in manifest (promotes the external repo's CI scaffold into Owlory as reusable templates).
Real third-party repo migration when a target is named explicitly by the user.
Generic git-command-failed smoke (5th and final ConfigError emit site).

Sync tool could grow a --force-templates flag that ignores the template-preserve guard, for consumers who want to deliberately re-baseline. Not implemented; consumer can delete then sync to achieve the same effect.
not_claimed: All template-flagged entries are equally safe under the new semantic. Three are now template-protected (prompts, examples, pyrightconfig); test coverage focuses on prompts. examples and pyrightconfig are presumed safe by the same code path but not directly asserted.
The new behavior is fully backward-compatible. Consumers who have already re-synced under the old behavior (and lost overrides) still need to re-establish their customizations once.
Real third-party repos have been migrated under the new semantic. The smoke test bootstraps temp consumers only.
residual_risks: Existing consumers may rely on --sync to refresh template files when Owlory updates them. The new behavior breaks that expectation; consumers must explicitly delete locals or merge manually. The docs name this trade-off; behavior change is intentional.

If a future contributor adds a non-template entry by mistake when they meant template, --sync will overwrite consumer customizations silently. The manifest is small enough that review catches this; a stricter contract (e.g., requiring template entries for any docs-like content) is out of scope.
recommended_next_reason: Queue is empty. Remaining boundaries: workflow templates in manifest, real third-party migration, generic-git-command-failed smoke. None queued.
